observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal
Accountant and Supply
Officer to:
a. Revisit the provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 22, 2022;
b. Collaborate and cause the immediate reconciliation of PPE records, reports, balances;
c. Upon reconciliation of PPE, identify the items that are below the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold and cause the immediate reclassification thereof;
d. Take appropriate actions as provided for in COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 22, 2022 such as, but not limited to: 1) Creation of Semi-Expendable Property Cards and Ledger Cards, 2) Preparation of Inventory Custodian Slips, and 3) Updating of Property Acknowledgement Receipts for those issued items.
e. Prepare a Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RegSPI)
f. Inform the Head of the Agency of the non-existing/missing PPEs without pending Request for Relief;
g. Henceforth comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2022-004.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
h. Effect necessary adjustments in the financial records and reports reconciled items based on the reconciliation with the Supply Officer.
i. Reflect the restated balances in the next financial reporting period with corresponding disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

1.7 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
1.7.1 Properly record Semi-Expendable Inventory and Semi-Expendable
Expenses for procured items below ₱50,000.00 as prescribed by
COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022, and COA Circular
No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024, on the proper classification and
accounting treatment, records to be maintained, and reports to be
submitted pertaining to semi-expendable properties;
1.7.2 Effect necessary accounting entries to correct as follows:
Account Title Debit Credit
Prior Period Adjustment ₱480,603.00
Semi-Expendable Office Equipment ₱400,528.00
Semi-Expendable Furniture and Fixtures ₱80,075.00
1.7.3 Periodically reconcile the issued items and henceforth observe the
correct recording and classification of expenses pertaining to the
PPE, Semi-Expendable Properties and related expenses.

2.6 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Engineer
to:
2.6.1 Justify the variances in the road lengths of completed projects listed in
Table II;
2.6.2 Submit as built plan for all completed projects;
2.6.3 Undertake monitoring activities during the implementation of
infrastructure projects by contract; and
2.6.4 Henceforth, prepare POW for attainable PPAs with realistic technical
specifications. Should the POW not be complied with, prepare and
submit a revised POW to conform to the current and ongoing status of
the PPA.

4.13 We recommended and Management agreed to:
4.13.1 Require the HRMO to conduct a thorough review of each
department’s staffing requirements and recommend hiring of
qualified personnel for regular appointments when necessary to
ensure regularization of Job Order (JO) employment and meet long-
term staffing needs;
4.13.2 Formalize employment contract with all JO workers by executing
contracts, clearly defining job descriptions, terms of employment,
compensation, areas of assignment, and work deliverables to be
accomplished;
4.13.3 Ensure that JO workers are hired strictly based on necessity and
urgency by assessing departmental needs and evaluating individual
assignments. This will prevent redundancy, duplication of tasks, and
inefficiencies in work accomplishments.

5.11 We recommended and Management agreed to:
5.11.1 Require the comply with the provisions of 6.2 of COA-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2, s.2020 dated October 20, 2020 and
pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR on the procurement
of consultancy services;
5.11.2 Submit the required monthly and annual reports as indicated in the
contracts of service the actual specific work delivered by the
consultants
5.11.3 Submit the proof of registration with the regulatory bodies such as
the SEC, DTI, CDA, BIR, as applicable, as well as the Mayor’s
permit where the place of registration of the consultant is situated;
and
5.11.4 Justify the necessity to hire their services duplicating their duties
and responsibilities of the consultants with the regular
employees/offices of the Municipality.

6.11 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Engineer
to:
6.11.1 Justify the employment of JOs relative to the implementation of the 30
infrastructure projects;
6.11.2 Submit JO contracts indicating the purpose of hiring, technical
skills/qualifications, period of service, and
6.11.3 Require each JO to submit individual accomplishment reports for each
pay period.
6.11.4 Henceforth, strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of Section 305
of RA 7160 and DPWH Department Orders on the preparation of POW
and Engineering and Administrative Overhead.

7.5 We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to:
7.5.1 Attribute portion of the LGU's regular programs and projects to the
current year’s GAD budget though the administration of Harmonized
Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG); and
7.5.2 If the LGU is not trained on the HGDG, cost of training with the gender
analysis specialist can be proposed in the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB)
for the ensuing budget year pursuant to Item 4, C, C.4, 5 (a,b) of the
PCW, DILG, DBM, NEDA JMC 2013-01.

8.5 We recommended and Management agreed to:
8.5.1 Direct the Municipal Treasurer:
8.5.1.1 To furnish the Municipal Accountant with duly certified list
of RPT due and collectible for the year;
8.5.2 Direct the Municipal Accountant:
8.5.2.1 To establish RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the
year using the duly certified list of taxpayers with the
amount due and collectible based on the
RPTAR/Taxpayer's Index;
8.5.2.2 Henceforth, comply with the NGAS Manual for LGUs and
IPSAS.

9.9 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
9.10 Municipal/Project Engineer to:
9.10.1 Conduct inspection and ascertain the project accomplishment status of
the PPAs per the Schedule of PPAs Still in Construction in Progress as
of December 31, 2024 (Annexes A and B) by indicating the following:
9.10.1.1 Percent of project completion if completed or not completed;
9.10.1.2 If not completed, submit justification for slow
implementation; and
9.10.1.3 If completed, issue a Certificate of Project Inspection, stating
that the completed project is per the approved Program of
Works, and submit a Certificate of Project Completion signed
by the Municipal Mayor.
9.10.2 Submit Project Status of On-going PPAs as of December 31, 2024, and
pictures of the PPAs per the Schedule of PPAs Still in Construction in
Progress (Annexes Q and R);
9.11 Municipal Accountant to:
9.11.1 Effect the reclassification of PPAs to their appropriate PPE accounts
upon receipt of the Certificate of Project Completion

We recommended and
Management agreed to
require:
The Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer, and
MDRRMO to:
a. Determine the correct amount of unexpended LDRRMF to be transferred to the Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account;
b. Avoid lumping the amounts for MOOE and CO under the 70% Mitigation Fund in the presentation of the Notes to Financial Statements; and
c. Ensure that all unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for Capital Outlay be made continuing in the General Fund books, without transferring the amount to Trust Liability, until the projects funded are completed.

We recommend that
Management require:
a. The contractor to immediately undertake the unperformed work items indicated above;
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit written justification as to why the contractor was paid the total amount of ₱0.750 million despite the unperformed work.

We recommended and the
Management agreed to direct
the Inventory Committee to plan and prepare and submit the
Approved Physical
Inventory Plan, signed by the Head of Agency, to the
COA Audit Team at least ten
(10) days before the
schedule of inventory-taking
activities, pursuant to COA
Circular 2020-006, to ensure
that all planned inventory
activities/procedures are
efficiently and effectively
performed by the Committee
members.

observations.reason: The same is still to be reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
Discontinue the use of the LDRRMF for administrative and operating expenditures; and


Ensure that all expenditures from the LDRRMF comply with the allowable expenses as prescribed by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and DBM-DILG-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.
observations.reason: The LDRRMF is still charged with recurring expenses such as water and electricity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require:
The Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer, and MDRRMO to:
Determine the correct amount of unexpended LDRRMF to be transferred to the Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account;
Avoid lumping the amounts for MOOE and CO under the 70% Mitigation Fund in the presentation of the Notes to Financial Statements; and
Ensure that all unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for Capital Outlay be made continuing in the General Fund books, without transferring the amount to Trust Liability, until the projects funded are completed.

We recommend that Management require:
The contractor to immediately undertake the unperformed work items indicated above; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit written justification as to why the contractor was paid the total amount of ₱0.750 million despite the unperformed work items.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee prepare and submit the Approved Physical Inventory Plan, signed by the Head of Agency, to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) days before the schedule of inventory-taking activities, pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006, to ensure that all planned inventory activities/procedures are efficiently and effectively performed by the Committee members.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the original signed distribution list for welfare goods given to constituents and institute control on various processes and secure appropriate documentation for claims of government funds.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Strengthen the collection of RPT/SET thru information or post the notice of the dates when the tax may be paid without interest at a conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the city or municipal hall. Said notice shall likewise be published in a newspaper of general circulation in the locality once a week for two (2) consecutive weeks (Section 249 of the same RA);

For delinquent accounts, send notices to delinquent taxpayer and cause the posting of the Notice of Delinquency pursuant to Section 254 of the same RA; and

Monitor and assess collection performance not only on the Performance Targets provided by oversight agencies but also on the actual RPT/SET due and collectible for the current year.

Require the Municipal Assessor to:

Coordinate with the Provincial Assessor for the revised valuation and assessment of real properties in the municipality pursuant to Section 219 of RA 7160 for an adjustment in the RPT and SET.

Require the SWM Board to:

Strengthen the 10-year SWM Plan through the formulation of the current year’s SWM Program containing all SWM PPAs, from waste reduction at source, collection, recycling, storage, processing, disposal, and other related SWM activities pursuant to Rule VI, Section 4(b) of the IRR of RA 9003;

Appropriate funds for the current year’s SWM PPA which shall be assigned in the MENRO for their effective and efficient implementation; and

Direct the MENRO Office to regularly supervise, monitor, and evaluate the implementation of all SWM activities.
observations.reason: Not implemented | The current funding is not expected to meet the demands of the SWM PPA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:


   Require the Inventory Committee to:


   a. Complete the physical inventory taking of PPE items in three months or
      less;


   b. Inform the COA Audit Team Leader of  the  scheduled  inventory  taking
      pursuant to Item 5.4 of COA Circular 2020 dated January 31, 2020; and

   c. Prepare the RPCPPE in four copies and submit the same to:

      c.1 Office of the Audit Team Leader;


      c.2 Accounting Unit


      c.3 Property Unit

We recommended that Management require the:


   a. LDRRMO to coordinate closely with the Municipal LDC in the formulation
      of a complete and integrated LDRRMP, formulate detailed  PPAs  and  to
      follow the timetable in the implementation of programmed PPAs; and

   b.  Refrain  from  using  the  LDRRMF  in  providing  appropriations  for
      administrative  related  expenses,  particularly  water   supply   and
      electricity expenses.

We recommended and the management agreed to:


   a. Transfer the unexpended balances for CYs 2014 and  2015  amounting  to
      ₱1.554 million to the GF pursuant to Item 5.1.13 of COA  Circular  No.
      2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and

   b. Report the balances of Continuing Appropriations in the Monthly Report
      on Utilization of LDRRMF to monitor PPAs that are on-going and/or  for
      implementation.

We recommended and the management agreed to:


   A. Require the Municipal Accountant to:


     a. Record procured inventory  items  to  their  appropriate  inventory
        accounts;


     b. Maintain  and  post  in  their  respective  Supplies  Ledger  Cards
        inventory items procured; and


     c. Reconcile with the inventory  of  welfare  goods  and  supplies  to
        ascertain discrepancies, if any.


   B. Require the Supply Officer to:


     a. Maintain and post in the Stock Cards the receipt and  issuances  of
        welfare goods and supplies based on the approved RIS issued;


     b. Prepare the Report of Supplies  and  Materials  Issued  (RSMI)  and
        submit the same to the  Accounting  for  the  recording  of  issued
        welfare goods and supplies supported with the RIS and  Distribution
        List of welfare goods given to beneficiaries;

     c. Take an inventory of welfare goods and supplies and reconcile with
        the Stock Cards to determine discrepancies, if any.

We recommended and the management agreed to:


   a. Require the MPDO and other concerned departments to formulate specific
      PPAs,  indicating   the   brief   description,   location,   time   of
      implementation, for every program  that  partakes  the  nature  of  an
      investment and capital expenditures;


   b. Coordinate with the MLDC in  the  formulation  of  specific  PPAs  for
      inclusion in the LDP and AIP;

   c. Ensure that the 20% DF is  optimally  utilized  to  help  achieve  the
      desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the
      LGU pursuant to Item 2.3 of DILG DBM  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No.
      2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

   d. Henceforth, require the  approval  or  authorization  from  the  Local
      Sanggunian prior to implementation of specific PPAs funded out of lump
      sum appropriation under the 20% DF pursuant to Article 4  (d)  of  the
      Rules and Regulation Implementing the Local Government Code (LGC);

We recommended and the management agreed to:


   A. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant:


     a. To record vaccines to their appropriate inventory accounts;


     b. To maintain and post to their respective Supplies Ledger Cards  the
        donated vaccines received; and


     c. To reconcile records with the inventory of vaccines received and on-
        hand to ascertain discrepancies, if any.


   B. Henceforth, the Supply Officer:


     a. To maintain and post in the Stock Cards the receipt  and  issuances
        of donated vaccines received;


     b. To prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued  (RSMI)  and
        submit the same to the  Accounting  for  the  recording  of  issued
        vaccines supported with the RIS and List of Vaccinated Individuals;

     c. To take an inventory of vaccines on hand  and  reconcile  with  the
        Stock Cards to determine discrepancies, if any.


   C. The MHO to reconcile with the PHO the number of vaccine doses received
             for the four brands of vaccine, namely,  Sinovac,  AstraZeneca,
      Pfizer and Gamaleya;


   D.  The  MHO  to  submit  the  utilization   report   on   vaccines/doses
      administered       for CY 2021 and the accurate number of vaccines  on
      hand as of          December 31, 2021.

a. Require the General Services Officer (GSO) Designate to immediate conduct physical inventory of all the PPE and submit to the AT the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and

observations.reason: Not Implemented due to the absence of the RPCPPE

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to immediately conduct physical inventory of
all the PPE and submit to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than January 31 of the
ensuing year; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate to maintain the
Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and the Property
Cards (PC), respectively, per category of PPE.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly the Bank Reconciliation
Statements for all bank accounts and submit to the Office of the Auditor
regularly, in compliance with Sections 3.1, 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-
011, to ensure the correctness of the bank account balances in the financial
statements;
b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain cashbooks for the two bank accounts
for easy conduct of reconciliation;
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to promptly secure bank statements or
snapshots on all bank accounts from depository banks on a monthly basis to
ensure the timely preparation of the bank reconciliation statements; and
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records regularly on all bank accounts of the Municipality to ensure the
correctness of the Cash in Bank accounts balances as reflected in the financial
statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of recording to direct
expense the purchases of supplies and materials for stock, consumption or
distribution and to record it as expense only upon distribution and utilization;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the perpetual inventory records
pursuant to Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for
Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I; and
c. Require the GSO designate to immediately prepare and submit to the Municipal
Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be
supported with the RIS.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the ORs be checked and verified against submitted Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs), in conformity with the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I, to ensure the correctness of the submitted reports and amount remitted.

observations.reason: BRS prepared and submitted were not complete

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should fast track the gathering of necessary documents for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts in order that necessary training for the personnel in-charged be started and for safe payment of salaries to employees thru ATM, in compliance to Sections 2 and 4(8) of PD No. 1445. Likewise, the reports being prepared and maintained by the DO be minimized and to avoid the risk of handling huge amount of cash and exposing them to possible loss through theft.

We recommended that Management give preferential attention on how to improve the Material Recovery Facility (MRF). Furthermore, enough funds should be provided for the salaries personnel and for the maintenance of its operation so that said facility could be utilized in accordance with Section 32 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003, to ensure the efficient and safe storage, sorting and processing of collected waste and to make sure that public health and environment are being protected.

In view of the following, we recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert the extra effort to determine the details of the above-mentioned receivable accounts and make the necessary actions to request for write-off if the details are impossible to establish, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: Request for write-off was not done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize the established Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in accordance with Section 32 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003, to ensure the efficient and safe storage, sorting and processing of collected waste and to make sure that the protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile his cashbooks with the accounting records pursuant to Item 7, Chapter II of the Revised Manual on Cash Examination, to ensure early discovery of errors and necessary adjustments could be effected immediately.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the General Services Officer (GSO) Designate to immediate conduct physical
inventory of all the PPE and submit to the AT the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and

b. Require the Municipal Accountant (MA) and the GSO Designate to reconcile their
records in order to determine discrepancies and adjustments be made.

a. That officials and employees concerned liquidate immediately the cash advances
granted them or refund the amount if the same could not be liquidated;

b. That demand letters be sent to the concerned officials and employees and if remained
unheeded, the LCE may cause or order the suspension of payment of their salaries
until they have fully liquidated thereof;

c. Follow-up the liquidation of the CIF from COA Central Office, and retrieve the
liquidation papers and furnish the same to the Auditor; and

d. Instruct the MA to monitor the liquidation of CAs to ensure that they are liquidated
within the reglementary period or as soon as the purpose for which it was given has
been served.

We recommended that the ORs be checked and verified against submitted Report of
Collections and Deposits (RCDs), in conformity with the New Government Accounting
System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I, to ensure the
correctness of the submitted reports and amount remitted.

We recommended that:

a. The ICO-MT reconcile his cashbooks with the accounting records pursuant to the
Revised Manual on Cash Examination, to ensure early discovery of errors and
necessary adjustments could be effected immediately;

b. The MA assign someone who would do the reconciliation on a regular basis to ensure
that balances reflected in the books are accurate and reliable; and

c. Require the MA to adjust all valid reconciling items that need to be adjusted and
corrected in the accounting books, after every cash examination.

a. The MA facilitate the timely preparation of the monthly TB and FS;

b. The MA prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same to the
Auditor, on a monthly basis to facilitate the early and timely discovery of errors and
discrepancies and to insure the accuracy and correctness of the cash accounts
reported in the financial statements of the Municipality; and

c. Require the MA to adjust all valid reconciling items that need to be adjusted and
corrected in the accounting books, after every cash examination.

We recommended that Management should fast track the gathering of necessary
documents for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts in order that necessary
training for the personnel in-charged be started and for safe payment of salaries to
employees thru ATM, in compliance to Sections 2 and 4(8) of PD No. 1445. Likewise, the
reports being prepared and maintained by the DO be minimized and to avoid the risk of
handling huge amount of cash and exposing them to possible loss through theft.

We recommended that Management give preferential attention on how to improve the
condition of the Material Recovery Facility (MRF). Furthermore, enough funds should be
provided for the salaries of personnel and for the maintenance of its operation so that
said facility could be utilized in accordance with Section 32 of Republic Act (RA) No.
9003, to ensure the efficient and safe storage, sorting and processing of collected waste
and to make sure that public health and environment are being protected.

In view of the following, we recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant to exert extra effort to determine the details of the above-mentioned
receivable accounts and make the necessary actions to request for write-off if the details
are impossible to establish, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.

a. Require the GSO to immediate conduct physical inventory of all the PPE and submit to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant (MA) and the GSO Designate to reconcile their records in order to determine discrepancies and adjustments be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant periodically provide depreciation for the PPEs recorded in the books of the Municipality, in conformity with Section 4(o) of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and PPSAS 17, to ensure correct presentation of PPE balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management follow-up the previous arrangement with the government bank for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Sections 2 and 4(8) of PD No. 1445 to avoid the risk of handling huge amount of cash and exposing them to possible loss through theft.

a. Allocate at least 1% of the IRA for the strengthening of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and implementation of pertinent PPAs; and
b. Strictly comply with the rules and regulations about the formulation of PPAs and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) be checked and verified, in conformity with Section 30 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I, to ensure the correctness of the amount remitted.
Likewise, we recommended the immediate remittance and deposit of the under collections.

a. Furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Regional Special Personnel Order (RSPO) extending the designation of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer (AMT) as ICO-Municipal Treasurer (ICO-MT), to ensure the legality of the transactions facilitated and authorized by the ICO-MT and to avoid being issued a Notice of Disallowance; and
b. Instruct the ICO-MT to stop signing disbursements in the absence of extension orders.

In view of the following, we recommended that the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to determine the details of the above-mentioned receivable accounts and make the necessary actions to request for write-off if the details are impossible to establish, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the PPEs recorded in the books of the Municipality, in conformity with Section 4(o) of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and PPSAS 17, to ensure correct presentation of PPE balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management follow-up the previous arrangement with the government bank for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Sections 2 and 4(8) of PD No. 1445 to avoid the risk of handling huge amount of cash and exposing them to possible loss through theft.

We recommended that Management:
Require the GSO to immediate conduct physical inventory of all the PPE and submit to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate to reconcile their records in order to determine discrepancies and adjustments be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant periodically provide depreciation for the PPEs recorded in the books of the Municipality, in conformity with Section 4(o) of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and PPSAS 17, to ensure correct presentation of PPE balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management follow-up the previous arrangement with the government bank for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Sections 2 and 4(8) of PD No. 1445 to avoid the risk of handling huge amount of cash and exposing them to possible loss through theft.

We recommended that Management utilize the established Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in accordance with Section 32 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003, to ensure the efficient and safe storage, sorting and processing of collected waste and to make sure the protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that Management:
Allocate at least 1% of the IRA for the strengthening of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and implementation of pertinent PPAs; and
Strictly comply with the rules and regulations about the formulation of PPAs and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) be checked and verified, in conformity with Section 30 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I,        to ensure the correctness of the amount remitted. Likewise, we recommended the immediate remittance and deposit of the under collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile his cashbooks with the accounting records pursuant to Item 7, Chapter II of the Revised Manual on Cash Examination, to ensure early discovery of errors and necessary adjustments could be effected immediately.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
Furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Regional Special Personnel Order (RSPO) extending the designation of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer (AMT) as ICO-Municipal Treasurer (ICO-MT) to ensure the legality of the transactions facilitated and authorized by the ICO-MT and to avoid being issued a Notice of Disallowance; and
Instruct the ICO-MT to stop signing disbursements in the absence of extension orders.

In view of the following, we recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to determine the details of the above-mentioned receivable accounts and make the necessary actions to request for write-off if the details are impossible to establish, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Require the GSO to immediate conduct physical inventory of all the PPE and submit to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and

Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate to reconcile their records in order to determine discrepancies and adjustments be made.

Refrain from releasing government funds to claims without complete documentations pursuant to the provision of Section 4(6) P.D. No. 1445 & Section 138(f) of GAAM; and 

Stop using the account “Subsidy to NGAs” for disbursements granted to individuals without determining the purpose for which it will be used.

Refrain from releasing government funds to claims without complete documentations pursuant to the provision of Section 4(6) P.D. No. 1445 & Section 138(f) of GAAM; and 

Stop recording in the “Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses” account, expenses that are not considered as other maintenance and operating expenses.

We recommended that the Municipality comply strictly with the provisions in Section 2.2 and Sub-sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.3 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, to ensure that the public is informed of any subsequent projects that will be implemented to foster transparency and accountability and also to encourage public participation.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the RCDs be checked and verified as to its completeness and correctness, in accordance with Section 30 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to avoid erroneous preparation of RCDs and to ensure timely detection and adjustment of errors.

We recommended that Management establish the Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in accordance with Section 32 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003, for efficient and safe storage, sorting and processing of collected waste and to ensure protection of public health and environment.

Allocate at least 1% of the IRA for the strengthening of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and implementation of pertinent PPAs; and

Strictly comply with the rules and regulations about the formulation of PPAs and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

Review and update the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and MF of the previous years; and 

Maximize the utilization of the current LDRRMF to strengthen and enhance the resilience of communities to disasters.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary reconciliation of the reported Trust Liability-DRRM before making the full transfer of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF for the previous years, as well as for the current year to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books; and

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the corresponding amount from the General Fund account to the Trust Fund account.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate to reconcile their records;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to book up unrecorded PPEs at appraised cost, and unaccounted PPE, if any, be verified.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the PPEs recorded in the books of the Municipality, in conformity with Section 4(o) of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and PPSAS 17, to ensure correct presentation of PPE balances in the financial statements.

a. Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through the bank, as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Sections 2 and 4(8) of PD 1445, with the expected result of minimizing the attendant risks identified with cash-paid payrolls and easing the effects of manpower attrition;
b. Make an arrangement with a government bank for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts by the employees through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and
c. Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the MOA.
a. Integrate specific PPAs in the AIP detailing every projects/activities to be undertaken with their corresponding budget in line with the 10-year ESWMP;
b. Prepare and submit accomplishment report to enable the Audit Team to properly monitor and evaluate the program in terms of its sustainability and effectiveness; and
c. Appropriate certain amount to fund the various PPAs programmed for SWM.

We recommended that Management:.
a. Require the GSO to immediate conduct physical inventory of all the PPE and submit
to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE); and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate to reconcile their records
in order to determine discrepancies and adjustments be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the PPEs
recorded in the books of the Municipality, in conformity with Section 4(o) of the Manual
on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and PPSAS 17, to ensure correct presentation of PPE
balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
c. Refrain from releasing government funds to claims without complete documentations
pursuant to the provision of Section 4(6) P.D. No. 1445 & Section 138(f) of GAAM;
and
d. Stop using the account “Subsidy to NGAs” for disbursements granted to individuals
without determining the purpose for which it will be used.

We recommended that Management:
a. Refrain from releasing government funds to claims without complete
documentations pursuant to the provision of Section 4(6) P.D. No. 1445 & Section
138(f) of GAAM; and
b. Stop recording in the “Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses” account,
expenses that are not considered as other maintenance and operating expenses.

We recommended that the Municipality comply strictly with the provisions in Section 2.2
and Sub-sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.3 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, to
ensure that the public is informed of any subsequent projects that will be implemented to
foster transparency and accountability and also to encourage public participation.

We recommended that the RCDs be checked and verified as to its completeness and
correctness, in accordance with Section 30 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to
avoid erroneous preparation of RCDs and to ensure timely detection and adjustment of
errors.

We recommended that Management follow-up the previous arrangement with the
government bank for the opening of individual ATM savings accounts as a means of
improving fiscal management in accordance with Sections 2 and 4(8) of PD No. 1445 to
avoid the risk of handling huge amount of cash and exposing them to possible loss
through theft.

We recommended that Management:
1. Integrate specific PPAs in the AIP detailing every projects/activities to be undertaken
with their corresponding budget in line with the 10-year ESWMP;
2. Appropriate certain amount to fund the various PPAs programmed for SWM; and
3. Prepare and submit accomplishment reportto enable the Audit Team toproperly
monitor and evaluate the program in terms of its sustainability and effectiveness.

We recommended that Management establish the Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in
accordance with Section 32 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003, for efficient and safe
storage, sorting and processing of collected waste and to ensure protection of public
health and environment.

We recommended that Management:
a. Allocate at least 1% of the IRA for the strengthening of the Local Councils for the
Protection of Children (LCPC) and implementation of pertinent PPAs; and
b. Strictly comply with the rules and regulations about the formulation of PPAs and
utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

We recommended that Management:
a. Review and update the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and include activities to be funded from the
unexpended QRF and MF of the previous years; and
b. Maximize the utilization of the current LDRRMF to strengthen and enhance the
resilience of communities to disasters.

We recommend that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary reconciliation of the reported
Trust Liability-DRRM before making the full transfer of the unexpended balances of
the LDRRMF for the previous years, as well as for the current year to the Special
Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books; and
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the corresponding amount from the
General Fund account to the Trust Fund account.
a. Instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to refrain from charging prior and current year’s transactions to Prior Period Adjustment;
b. Make sure that prior year’s transactions are set up as Accounts Payable;
c. Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
a. Create a committee to conduct physical inventory of all the PPEs owned by the Municipality and submit to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE);
We recommended that Management return the unexpended balance of completed projects to the NGA concerned to finance other developmental projects.

We recommended that Management require the immediate implementation of the above identified projects received from various NGAs and submit the required reports to the SA for proper accounting of the funds released to IA. Henceforth, monitor and undertake the projects received through fund transfer without delay and in accordance with the work plan and schedule, otherwise, return the unutilized funds to the NGAs concerned to be used for other developmental projects.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that at the end of each business day, all collecting officers be required to accomplish the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD), submit it to the Revenue Liquidating Officer, together with the duplicate copies of the official receipts issued and turn over all collected.

We recommended that collectors be required to issue the pre-numbered official receipts in strict numerical sequence in accordance with Section 73 of the GAAM, Volume I and the Local Treasury Operations Manual to ensure that all collections are remitted on time and to avoid misapplication and misuse of the same.

We recommended that Management refrain from utilizing the 20% DF for payment of expenses not related to developmental projects, pursuant to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, series 2011 dated April 13, 2011.
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management prepare the LSB budget in accordance with the timeframe set in Section 7 of RA No. 5447.

We recommended that the Municipality utilize the unexpended Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF totalling ₱5.070 million and funds from other sources totalling ₱26,961.00 to ensure that basic rescue and response equipment were procured so as to build the disaster resilience of communities and to institutionalize arrangements and measures for reducing disaster risks.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to refrain from charging prior and current
year’s transactions to Prior Period Adjustment;
b. Make sure that prior year’s transactions are set up as Accounts Payable; and
c. Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments, if
warranted.

We recommended that Management:
a. Create a committee to conduct physical inventory of all the PPEs owned by the
Municipality and submit to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE);
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate to reconcile their records;
and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to book up unrecorded PPEs at appraised cost,
and unaccounted PPE, if any, be verified.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the PPEs
recorded in the books of the Municipality, in conformity with Section 4(o) of the Manual
on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and PPSAS 17, to ensure correct presentation of PPE
balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management return the unexpended balance of completed
projects to the NGA concerned to finance other developmental projects.

We recommended that Management require the immediate implementation of the above
identified projects received from various NGAs and submit the required reports to the
SA for proper accounting of the funds released to IA. Henceforth, monitor and undertake
the projects received through fund transfer without delay and in accordance with the
work plan and schedule, otherwise, return the unutilized funds to the NGAs concerned
to be used for other developmental projects.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that at the end of each business day, all
collecting officers be required to accomplish the Report of Collections and Deposits
(RCD), submit it to the Revenue Liquidating Officer, together with the duplicate copies of
the all official receipts issued and turn over all cash collected.

We recommended that collectors be required to issue the pre-numbered official receipts
in strict numerical sequence in accordance with Section 73 of the GAAM, Volume I and
the Local Treasury Operations Manual to ensure that all collections are remitted on time
and to avoid misapplication and misuse of the same.

We recommended that Management refrain from utilizing the 20% DF for payment of
expenses not related to developmental projects, pursuant to the provisions of DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, series 2011 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that Management:
a) Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other
emoluments through the bank, as a means of improving fiscal management in
accordance with Sections 2 and 4(8) of PD 1445, with the expected result of
minimizing the attendant risks identified with cash-paid payrolls and easing the
effects of manpower attrition;
b) Make an arrangement with a government bank for the opening of individual ATM
savings accounts by the employees through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA);
and
c) Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the MOA.

a. Stop utilizing SEF for purposes that are not in accordance with Sec.100(c) and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 01, series of 1998 dated April 14, 1998; and
b. Prioritize programs and activities in line with the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the payrolls and effect the necessary adjustments on the deductions of those receiving below P3,000.00, in the order of preference and to inform the employee concerned to make proper arrangements with the other lending institutions on repayments so as not to reduce their net take home pay below than what was prescribed under the General Provisions of the GAA of FY 2015.
We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant be prudent in issuing the Certificate of Net Take Home Pay to their employees, which is one of the requisites when applying for a loan, to avoid the granting of loans to those employees who no longer have the capacity to pay.

observations.managementAction: The new Municipal Mayor assured the Audit Team of the implementation of the recommendations.
